originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and watch scripts, then initializes many inline watch-movement scenes under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, route-local watch model assets, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell, the watch model data, and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the opening, spring, automatic-winding, and final-size scenes remain interactive.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with a draggable assembled movement view, then steps through coil spring, torsion spring, mainspring, winding, and barrel scenes with linked sliders and animated cross-sections.
localStatus: The local route keeps the opening movement scene and the early spring and winding demonstrations under the original published runtime.
notes: The smoke contract treats one opening slider path and one early power scene as required parity behavior.
The route preserves the published progression from the assembled movement into the mainspring and barrel mechanics instead of flattening those sections into static diagrams.
evidence: Post-engine rollout planning called for one movement scene and one early power or spring slider path.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the opening hero scene and coil-spring scene update from slider input.

originalBehavior: The published article continues through the gear train, escapement, balance, motion works, date, keyless works, and automatic winding sections with linked 3D views, highlighted parts, and synchronized slider updates.
localStatus: The local route keeps the later automatic winding, reversing wheel, and keyless-works scenes intact through the original article canvas and slider runtime.
notes: The published 3D watch scenes now load their geometry from the route-local model tree instead of the upstream root path.
The shipped `gears` references are rewritten locally in the public body.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one later automatic-winding or escapement scene and one final-size scene as parity-critical for the watch batch.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the automatic-behavior scene and credit-card-size scene update from slider input without runtime asset failures.

originalBehavior: The published article closes with further-reading sections and an upstream footer that includes supporter messaging and author provenance, while also referring back to other Bartosz articles by upstream URL.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published closing sections while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface and rewriting available local cross-links.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The public body no longer includes the Patreon footer prompt or other author-promo surfaces.
evidence: The hardened Ciechanowski audit flags ordinary in-body upstream article links while allowing footer-only provenance.
Route cleanup rewrote the local `gears` links so the family continues converging toward local intra-family navigation.
